Friends, Donald Trump and his MAGA allies are TERRIFIED of this race. Why? Because they know Florida’s 6th Congressional District is their Achilles’ heel. With a razor-thin majority in the House, losing this seat would destroy their plans to push Trump’s Project 2025 agenda through Congress. _**As a Democrat running to flip this seat, I know firsthand that this district is ready for a change.**_ Book bans. Attacks on reproductive rights. Gutting public schools. That’s their vision for the next two years—and it’s up to us to stop it. This race is Trump’s nightmare because it’s not just about one seat. A Democratic win here would derail his agenda and show the nation that MAGA extremism is losing its grip on power. But here’s the thing: they’re pouring dark money into this race, and every dollar is designed to drown out our grassroots movement. They’re betting we won’t fight back. [**Will you prove them wrong? Chip in $25, $50, or whatever you can today -- split with Serve America -- to help us flip this seat and deliver the first blow to Trump’s MAGA agenda.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102007079/527783572/-423390815?refcode=250212_Serve_Weil_TrumpNightmare&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTBmMThmYS02OWU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rN4TSEQ31l7UX4_IN2imKzZ5Y0BuQwidAby86uXi36s=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102007080/527783573/-2031701896?refcode=250212_Serve_Weil_TrumpNightmare&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTBmMThmYS02OWU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rN4TSEQ31l7UX4_IN2imKzZ5Y0BuQwidAby86uXi36s=) This isn’t just about politics. Gratefully,
Josh [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102007081/527783574/-423390815?refcode=250212_Serve_Weil_TrumpNightmare&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTBmMThmYS02OWU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rN4TSEQ31l7UX4_IN2imKzZ5Y0BuQwidAby86uXi36s=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102007082/527783575/-2031701896?refcode=250212_Serve_Weil_TrumpNightmare&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNTBmMThmYS02OWU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rN4TSEQ31l7UX4_IN2imKzZ5Y0BuQwidAby86uXi36s=)

| You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[ unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T85d8fcba-80a1-40b6-800a-c7d134208b01/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _._

| | | | | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf919c635-60d1-4e7a-9864-6892a5b7c2e2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Unelected, unaccountable and out of control billionaires are buying power and influence in Trump’s Washington, Peter. What kind of America are we trying to create? Housing costs are too high. Grocery costs are too high. Insurance costs are too high. Childcare costs are too high. Utility costs are too high. America is too expensive, Peter. It is not acceptable – in the wealthiest country in the history of the world -- for countless families to be forced to live paycheck to paycheck. As Trump's Billionaire-First agenda takes shape and his wealthy benefactors claim exclusive access to our democracy, Democrats are the only ones committed to lowering costs for hardworking taxpayers. Here's my question for you, Peter: Are we going to allow Republicans to spend the next four years building a country of the billionaires, by the billionaires and for the billionaires? Or are we going to stand together, build a strong foundation to fight back against the greed and corruption of extreme MAGA Republicans and the billionaire class? [Democrats have already decided, Peter: We will not sell out working people, families and seniors to give the ultra-wealthy a tax handout. We can't secure a more affordable future by ourselves. We need your support. Will you split a $20 donation between Blue Amp Action and the DCCC to help build an unbeatable Democratic movement, mobilize voters and flip the House blue? >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1dfb232c-0f3c-4ee0-851f-d74c17df46af/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal polls and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country.

name: Britney VanCitters
subject: Join our Storytellers Workshop!
body:
How you can help us fight back against high utility bills...| | | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102007769/527786239/-989641858?contactdata=fZjFkwQVKENJrM7Vc7H8XmyFyHDVakAL/WZTtdx82S7t1sdYheoc/+Sh98avTcEQ8p7tpmSE7lh1NKSK6ddHffhwtcDBKWXkhoR4Nmv+LIoPbk0379dfSqi1N1oLyEfV&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY2Y2I3NzNhLTZiZTktZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=4pQ8egole7LnMiZpw81UTWHEa0z4BN1CnxugH-gvQnc=&emci=194fa2a5-b2e8-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=66cb773a-6be9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=2107230)
---
Dear Peter, Climate change is here, and our communities are already feeling the impacts. On top of that, utility rate hikes in Oregon have gone too far, which makes us less resilient and safe. That’s why OLCV helped co-found [Building Resilience](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102007770/527786240/-1480106498?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY2Y2I3NzNhLTZiZTktZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=4pQ8egole7LnMiZpw81UTWHEa0z4BN1CnxugH-gvQnc=&emci=194fa2a5-b2e8-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=66cb773a-6be9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=2107230) in 2023, a statewide coalition of climate justice, public health, business, and labor organizations; and thousands of individual Oregonians advocating for legislative action to promote healthy resilient communities that run on affordable clean energy. The 2025 legislative session is in full swing, and that means the Building Resilience Coalition’s bills could be up for a public hearing any day now! Oregonians like you, as well as clean energy contractors and practitioners, can make a huge difference by sharing your clean energy and climate resilience stories. We know that when it comes to skyrocketing utility costs, and climate resilience everyone has a story to share. We also know that finding your story, crafting it, and sharing it is a skill. That’s why we’re hosting a Storytelling Workshop! At this Building Resilience campaign training, you’ll learn the basics of storytelling, get some tips and tricks, and meet members of our team who are here to support you — **[JOIN US!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102007771/527786242/-989641858?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkxDVi8xLzcwODc3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY2Y2I3NzNhLTZiZTktZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=4pQ8egole7LnMiZpw81UTWHEa0z4BN1CnxugH-gvQnc=&emci=194fa2a5-b2e8-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=66cb773a-6be9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=2107230)** **Building Resilience Storytelling Workshop****When:** Tuesday, February 18th from 6:00 - 7:00 PM**Where:** Zoom (link provided to RSVPs)

clean_body: How you can help us fight back against high utility bills...| | | Dear Peter, Climate change is here, and our communities are already feeling the impacts. On top of that, utility rate hikes in Oregon have gone too far, which makes us less resilient and safe. That’s why OLCV helped co-found in 2023, a statewide coalition of climate justice, public health, business, and labor organizations; and thousands of individual Oregonians advocating for legislative action to promote healthy resilient communities that run on affordable clean energy. The 2025 legislative session is in full swing, and that means the Building Resilience Coalition’s bills could be up for a public hearing any day now! Oregonians like you, as well as clean energy contractors and practitioners, can make a huge difference by sharing your clean energy and climate resilience stories. We know that when it comes to skyrocketing utility costs, and climate resilience everyone has a story to share. We also know that finding your story, crafting it, and sharing it is a skill. That’s why we’re hosting a Storytelling Workshop! At this Building Resilience campaign training, you’ll learn the basics of storytelling, get some tips and tricks, and meet members of our team who are here to support you — Building Resilience Storytelling WorkshopWhen: Tuesday, February 18th from 6:00 - 7:00 PMWhere: Zoom (link provided to RSVPs) | | This year, Building Resilience has three priorities to reduce energy costs and support Oregonians in making the energy efficiency and resilience upgrades they want. One Stop Shop 2.0 (HB 3081) Get the Junk Out of Rates (SB 88) * Fully Funding Climate Resilience Oregon’s legislature must take action in 2025 to give more Oregonians access to more resilient and safer homes and buildings. See you there! name: Team Tina
subject: Defeating MAGA momentum
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
|
#
Team — After almost a month of Donald Trump’s second administration, Trump and his team have made it clear: _they think they have MAGA momentum_. From the executive orders ripping away our rights and freedoms to the efforts by GOP members of Congress to make cuts to Social Security, Medicare, and the _Affordable Care Act_ , the MAGA agenda is alive and well. **This is why Tina is fighting so hard to defeat extremism and get our country back to work for the American people.** When Republicans tried to push the architect of Project 2025 through confirmation, Tina and Senate Democrats held the Senate floor all night to protest and call attention to the threats. When Donald Trump wanted to cut funding to federal programs, Tina and Senate Democrats got loud about the impacts on Medicaid, Head Start, and other programs that affect everyday Americans. name: Deb Haaland
subject: I have exciting news, folks
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate
body:
I’m launching my campaign for Governor of the great state of New Mexico.

You received this message from Lisa Blunt Rochester because you've previously taken action and supported Democrats like Lisa. If you would like to get less email, **[click here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102009622/527792255/1079316600?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.02.12_Haaland_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmE1MGM1MC02ZGU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPQrAbBTJUskmUohC-SP4usKbF5YAtOLLn3my4mCjt8=&emci=e0e02a5a-aae8-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=aba50c50-6de9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=11913588)** To unsubscribe, **[click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/_6DeEby01kOvxTWj8qzW6A2?unsubscribedata=fxE4AT3exv%2FWfM%2BtK%2FNraF80XR29VSixCZIafGNMZ02ZEl3O3nyupO1uhGhYJClC%2BYt5rAxSCqeXKHhgp0FqHNU79fyccfCAomzpq3JnrPsCsULakiYqnRymuyaFOAQqOAZHhWqDj5WYZvo2MfniI8oUjHK%2FPWlaV5fv7UUVUwBmNgmDQjME52dtjMwT29%2BRmQmnWWiMhz4j65j2awjmZ3WWEcvXN5ykII1K5RvBbLfvHkOM08ZJVu5I4ikGiSX53eOIEcB2zjChZsjDqDVe0IEa6Fn9UOga9FHg8WR8FLcGhVy%2FkDgyV0YkCnpG5Rh9lKDeWmJWon3hs7DvqEqFz1wYXc2tlNzrNzIR6iguR8NVuQOfFo7tLLhARSoKZj3FCfn79yh3jmcgys0QplXd1GLpX1eQbBCtoH7HsMoWzOwnmFoR4p4M9UamNvzq6vVh)**

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102009623/527792256/631174349?refcode=LOC_LBR_EM_FR_2024_Email-Header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmE1MGM1MC02ZGU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPQrAbBTJUskmUohC-SP4usKbF5YAtOLLn3my4mCjt8=)
---
Lisa Blunt Rochester said I could reach out with some exciting news, folks: I’m launching my campaign for Governor of the great state of New Mexico — my home — to bring our state together and build a bright future for all who live here. I’m humbly asking: [**_Will you join us as a week-one donor by donating just $3 or more to my campaign and Lisa Blunt Rochester now?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102009624/527792257/-1535617658?amount=3&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.02.12_Haaland_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmE1MGM1MC02ZGU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPQrAbBTJUskmUohC-SP4usKbF5YAtOLLn3my4mCjt8=) The amount you give doesn’t matter. What matters is building a campaign with the support of real people. That’s who I am and who I came from. That’s who I am running to serve. My roots in New Mexico run 35 generations deep. The Pueblo people were the first farmers in the high deserts of New Mexico, an agricultural tradition that still continues today. I am here because of my grandfather, who worked for decades as a diesel mechanic on the railroad. Somehow he also kept a field — picking worms off the corn as it grew, like the generations before him. I am here because of my grandmother, a survivor and a caretaker who woke up every day before the sun. She was the one who gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. (She wouldn’t let us in the kitchen while she cooked, but I’d perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours.) I am here because of my parents and their sacrifice. My mother, a Pueblo woman, served in the Navy. My father, a Marine, received a Silver Star for his service in Vietnam. He now lies in honor at Arlington. I am here because of my beloved child, who I raised on my own. I started a small business to pay the bills, but it still wasn’t always enough. We relied on food stamps to get on our feet. I sometimes had to decide between paying rent and buying groceries to feed our family. I will never forget how that felt or the kindness of those who helped us – strangers who became community. When I look at my Somah, I see perseverance, strength and hope. These are my stories. These are stories of New Mexico. These days, our differences are blown so far out of proportion. We lose sight of a simple fact: we all live in the same state, together. All of us in New Mexico come from love, survival, and sacrifice. We all want to live good lives — and we want even more for our children. The current president and his billionaire friends look at the lands and the people of this country and think: what can I exploit? I am running to ask: if we come together in New Mexico, what good can we do for each other and for the next generation? We have our work cut out for us. The cost of living is crushing our families. Our economy is growing, but we need to make sure everyone shares in our progress. Our families need more. Our farmers and ranchers need more. New Mexico’s children need so much more. And we must be good stewards of the Earth, and preserve the rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests whose beauty sustains our communities. I am running to do all this and more. I am running to serve all New Mexicans and make sure we’re leaving a better future for our children. [**_Will you join us with a week one donation, split between my campaign and Lisa Blunt Rochester?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102009625/527792258/-1535617658?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.02.12_Haaland_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmE1MGM1MC02ZGU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPQrAbBTJUskmUohC-SP4usKbF5YAtOLLn3my4mCjt8=) I would be honored to have your support. Many thanks to Lisa Blunt Rochester for helping spread the word. I hope you’ll join us, folks! Be fierce, Deb
* * *
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102009626/527792259/631174349?refcode=LOC_LBR_EM_FR_2024_Email-Footer&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYmE1MGM1MC02ZGU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPQrAbBTJUskmUohC-SP4usKbF5YAtOLLn3my4mCjt8=) I officially became the first woman and person of color to **_ever_** represent Delaware in the Senate. clean_body: I’m launching my campaign for Governor of the great state of New Mexico. You received this message from Lisa Blunt Rochester because you've previously taken action and supported Democrats like Lisa. If you would like to get less email, To unsubscribe, | | = Lisa Blunt Rochester said I could reach out with some exciting news, folks: I’m launching my campaign for Governor of the great state of New Mexico — my home — to bring our state together and build a bright future for all who live here. I’m humbly asking: = The amount you give doesn’t matter. What matters is building a campaign with the support of real people. That’s who I am and who I came from. That’s who I am running to serve. My roots in New Mexico run 35 generations deep. The Pueblo people were the first farmers in the high deserts of New Mexico, an agricultural tradition that still continues today. I am here because of my grandfather, who worked for decades as a diesel mechanic on the railroad. Somehow he also kept a field — picking worms off the corn as it grew, like the generations before him. I am here because of my grandmother, a survivor and a caretaker who woke up every day before the sun. She was the one who gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. (She wouldn’t let us in the kitchen while she cooked, but I’d perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours.) I am here because of my parents and their sacrifice. My mother, a Pueblo woman, served in the Navy. My father, a Marine, received a Silver Star for his service in Vietnam. He now lies in honor at Arlington. I am here because of my beloved child, who I raised on my own. I started a small business to pay the bills, but it still wasn’t always enough. We relied on food stamps to get on our feet. I sometimes had to decide between paying rent and buying groceries to feed our family. I will never forget how that felt or the kindness of those who helped us – strangers who became community. When I look at my Somah, I see perseverance, strength and hope. These are my stories. These are stories of New Mexico. These days, our differences are blown so far out of proportion. We lose sight of a simple fact: we all live in the same state, together. All of us in New Mexico come from love, survival, and sacrifice. We all want to live good lives — and we want even more for our children. The current president and his billionaire friends look at the lands and the people of this country and think: what can I exploit? I am running to ask: if we come together in New Mexico, what good can we do for each other and for the next generation? We have our work cut out for us. The cost of living is crushing our families. Our economy is growing, but we need to make sure everyone shares in our progress. Our families need more. Our farmers and ranchers need more. New Mexico’s children need so much more. And we must be good stewards of the Earth, and preserve the rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests whose beauty sustains our communities. I am running to do all this and more. I am running to serve all New Mexicans and make sure we’re leaving a better future for our children. = I would be honored to have your support. Many thanks to Lisa Blunt Rochester for helping spread the word. I hope you’ll join us, folks! Be fierce, Deb | = I officially became the first woman and person of color to ever represent Delaware in the Senate. But even though our campaign is over, the work has just begun. Will you chip in any amount today to help Bright Hope take center stage in the Senate? | = = Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate PO BOX 9767 Wilmington, DE 19809 United States | mailto:info@lisabluntrochester.com Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate Emails like these are an important aspect of our strategy to keep in touch with our supporters. If you want to receive fewer emails, If you wish to unsubscribe, please**

name: Ben Samuels
subject: Robinhood is evil
body:
So are DraftKings and FanDuel. All three apps make it way too easy to become gambling addicts.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.I938Xz5upE8oHgzk1NWRJOIB9FLiksju6aW9_8rxDSI?) for more
---
# [Robinhood is evil](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=156528893&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTI4ODkzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzkzODQ1MjYsImV4cCI6MTc0MTk3NjUyNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aIV9dIb0rxlnBXSto5cLgls7XC6iLixiou3cOoES8K4)
### So are DraftKings and FanDuel. All three apps make it way too easy to become gambling addicts.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Feb 12
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack..cMiClrRm-KTLrQa1AVUGPQ50fkY9vIUyy9TYcabyR54?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/robinhood-is-evil?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

A few months ago, [I encouraged Missourians to vote against Amendment 2](https://substack.com/redirect/72e99f3c-b0e6-47b2-ae58-0c210a8e490f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the basis that online sports gambling is dangerous.
The pro-gambling funders--namely DraftKings and FanDuel--[spent $34 million more](https://substack.com/redirect/7d851afe-6ddb-47ee-aa0c-8e903d57eb6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)1 than the opposition, and the [amendment passed with a 0.1% margin](https://substack.com/redirect/5f41f099-8115-4b7b-b054-0797fe025d3c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Nonetheless, mobile sports gambling remains a huge problem. So last week, when [Robinhood](https://substack.com/redirect/6509304c-c067-4c72-a83f-038db7e82686?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) said that it would [allow sports gambling through its app](https://substack.com/redirect/f2a32c64-5bd3-4e04-9cfa-ef125c6ce849?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), that piqued my interest.2
**Platforms like Robinhood** 3**are making investing more like gambling, peddling what amount to get-rich-quick schemes.** People are saving less and [becoming gambling addicts](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). **And by turning all of it into a game, people are ruining their lives.**
[ Subscribe now](https://substack.com/redirect/0121fbbc-4f77-489a-9f4e-a162e820c249?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### A reminder: mobile sports gambling is very bad
A majority of states have [legalized mobile sports gambling](https://substack.com/redirect/0b8563d1-7678-43bf-8005-25e4dc5cea43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since a [Supreme Court ruling in 2018 gave them the right to do so](https://substack.com/redirect/d1b957d5-5683-40a9-bac2-53a85b00c658?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
When states legalize online sports gambling, [per-person gambling spend increases by more than 6×](https://substack.com/redirect/3d394681-2637-42ec-a060-177b8c643eeb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But even beyond that, what we're seeing in those states [is consistently frightening](https://substack.com/redirect/8c05e9cc-87b1-4ae5-aed1-b85cf138e865?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
* [Gambling addiction is up](https://substack.com/redirect/77d097f6-71eb-4398-9821-05fc147ca686?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), potentially by as much as 30%. Gambling addiction--and frighteningly, this is not an exaggeration--[engages the brain in much the same way that a heroin addiction does](https://substack.com/redirect/b753f712-6130-4d73-ab79-b8872d88edea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).4
* [Credit scores are down and bankruptcy filings are ](https://substack.com/redirect/e902ad7d-4207-450b-8fcc-8830acb927fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[way](https://substack.com/redirect/e902ad7d-4207-450b-8fcc-8830acb927fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ up](https://substack.com/redirect/e902ad7d-4207-450b-8fcc-8830acb927fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).5 This is especially true among [men and among younger people](https://substack.com/redirect/28a2a7e7-aef3-4598-b9e5-1d454c603813?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--many of whom aren't old enough to legally gamble, but are old enough to [gamble away their student loan money](https://substack.com/redirect/fe020258-a940-40a2-82b6-5fad2f46de44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).6
* Mobile sports gambling leads to [higher rates of domestic violence](https://substack.com/redirect/f849b13e-4a0f-414f-a40c-18364fb395b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [higher rates of suicide](https://substack.com/redirect/86ea07aa-c957-4e8e-8286-5b38cc82c3d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Basically no one is making money on these apps. According to an [extensive study that included more than 700,000 people](https://substack.com/redirect/e8963d42-3239-4e9a-8cd0-dfa69ca806f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "fewer than 5% withdrew more from their betting apps than they deposited."

DraftKings and FanDuel know all of this, of course. And I have no hesitation calling both companies evil, in terms of the willfully negative impact they have on society.7
### The blurry line between mobile sports gambling and Robinhood "investing"
Here's where Robinhood comes in. They're not just trying to get into sports gambling. **Robinhood is masquerading gambling as investing** , encouraging its customers to take outlandish, irresponsibly high-risk bets on its platform. I can't sum it up any [better than ](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[The Wall Street Journal](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ :
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
To a remarkable degree, everything Robinhood does mimics some of the worst behaviors of online sports gambling.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=156528893&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU2NTI4ODkzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzkzODQ1MjYsImV4cCI6MTc0MTk3NjUyNiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aIV9dIb0rxlnBXSto5cLgls7XC6iLixiou3cOoES8K4)
### How Robinhood plays into addictive behaviors and gambling
Robinhood users are [disproportionately young and male](https://substack.com/redirect/99cb6641-8d10-4104-84e0-33e89709d490?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--as we've established, exactly the population most susceptible to gambling addiction.
1. As Scott Galloway put it, "**[Robinhood has gamified online trading into an addiction](https://substack.com/redirect/59146f61-6c31-405c-96de-eb182b6bfd1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**." They're deploying a lot of the same tactics and tricks that get you onto the app, and keep you on the app, that social media and online gambling platforms use.8
2. [Predictions markets](https://substack.com/redirect/b11c861c-f99e-4e52-b503-a60bab2cfd80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--the ability to wager on certain real-world outcomes--[opened on Robinhood ahead of the 2024 election](https://substack.com/redirect/8232b94e-7577-4733-a6ad-caebc9b6eb1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). _The Verge_ summarized this best: "[Robinhood admits it's just a gambling app](https://substack.com/redirect/2fea08a7-7d2a-42c5-8300-978a29f66cce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
3. I've got my own thoughts on cryptocurrency,9 but recent studies have shown a "[likely relationship between problem gambling and cryptocurrency trading](https://substack.com/redirect/adfe4703-8028-4c09-948f-aee22053a592?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."10 It's hardly the only place to trade crypto, but **Robinhood is making an[increasingly substantial portion of its revenue on crypto trading](https://substack.com/redirect/6b1704fb-cab4-4206-8553-21c8bd88df62?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**11
4. Robinhood largely makes its money by [taking a small percentage of every individual trade](https://substack.com/redirect/9791686b-024e-46a5-98f0-8ac8a097be03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).12 This sets up misaligned incentives: trading at high volumes is not consistent with how people should be investing and saving.13
5. Between the [Robinhood Investor Index](https://substack.com/redirect/76df71e4-1238-4874-b47f-9accef3c0a07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and other indicators on the app, they strongly bias users towards "hot" stocks.14 **[The people buying the most popular stocks on Robinhood end up systematically losing money](https://substack.com/redirect/d0941955-3a22-4f70-81e7-3ea046e6f1d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** : -4.7% over the subsequent 20-day period, which becomes substantial when it compounds.

For most of us, investing is--and should be!--saving for retirement and/or for a rainy day fund. That generally means that we shouldn't be making trades that involve wild day-to-day swings that risk cleaning us out entirely. But Robinhood makes that part of its core platform of offerings.
In short: everything about Robinhood reads a little bit like this fake alcohol ad from _South Park_ , with a faux-warning at the end that rings entirely hollow.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d2c0311f-a848-4297-8a2b-dfcb96327e9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### Unlike Robin Hood, Robinhood steals from the poor
[In an interview last year](https://substack.com/redirect/c3ef57f6-631e-4579-b1a4-00aa94966a30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Robinhood CEO [Vlad Tenev](https://substack.com/redirect/a8dbfddf-3078-4b8b-a512-d3b99f9d680e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) talked about the company's long-stated goal of opening up finance to everyone.
In certain respects, [he's correct](https://substack.com/redirect/dca078dc-d4d6-4257-b7f7-8a586372415c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that there's a lot about investing that isn't egalitarian: access to private markets, access to venture investing, etc. is limited.15 Democratizing investments would be good!
But **Robinhood isn 't doing that because they aren't promoting savings and investment. They're promoting gambling.** It's no accident that [addiction centers are seeing such marked increases in patients seeking help for trading](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).16
Sports gambling is relatively easy to regulate; regulating how people gamble on platforms like Robinhood is much harder. Do we want to wholesale ban people from options trading? No, probably not. But do we want most Americans putting their retirement savings in options? Very clearly not.17
This TikTok video shows us [how easy it is to start options trading](https://substack.com/redirect/7db30a66-23c0-4d5c-af33-dd15cd8e4b04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on Robinhood. (Namely, by lying about your expertise.) And that, along with a million other issues, is a real problem.
### Make investing boring again
I've written before about how [hard it is to be a young man today](https://substack.com/redirect/629f17f1-2b93-45e6-8593-477756b954a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and gambling platforms like Robinhood and DraftKings are making that problem much worse.18
At the same time, [trust in the financial services industry is among the lowest of any industry](https://substack.com/redirect/2e4b010b-73da-4dc4-8523-a947359ed6e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And [a majority of Americans believe that the stock market is rigged against them](https://substack.com/redirect/f39aa352-8115-4997-a158-413b9a0f9720?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg),19 even though returns for decades have demonstrated that it's part of a path to retirement for the [100s of millions of Americans with stock market exposure](https://substack.com/redirect/d8d66ce9-4c1b-4e1a-ad3d-8d163a37c87c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
How do we solve this?
1. _Regulation_ : Outlaw gamification that encourages big bets and irresponsible investments. Restrict notifications that encourage the sort of reactionary behavior that has people [buying high and selling low](https://substack.com/redirect/d0941955-3a22-4f70-81e7-3ea046e6f1d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
2. _Separation_ : Don't allow people to invest and gamble (on sports or prediction markets) in the same account. It should not be that easy to move money from one to the other, and the two shouldn't be treated as equivalents.
3. _Education_ : [Teach financial literacy in school](https://substack.com/redirect/431cee25-67de-4c1a-9095-70514e30c8ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Explain the power of compound interest. Help people understand that what they're seeing on Robinhood and elsewhere--something they're exposed to at an increasingly young age--is gambling, not investing.

People will always want to get rich quick; that's an instinct as old as time. But the more we can do to keep people from recklessly gambling--something that's become a Robinhood forte--the better off we'll all be.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTU2NTI4ODkzLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzM5Mzg0NTI2LCJleHAiOjE3NDE5NzY1MjYsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.lwIrqWp7DMoAby9l4g0WykTTkeuVsy4j1gHb6PyZp3g?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/0121fbbc-4f77-489a-9f4e-a162e820c249?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)_
_For press inquiries, please contact press@ben-samuels.com._
1
For context, they spent about 4.7× more--and not for nothing, ran a better campaign--than the opposition. Having Hall of Famer and former Cardinal [Ozzie Smith](https://substack.com/redirect/5c4a7173-271e-48b1-8c85-6d2793a8f15b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who remains immensely popular, [appear in St. Louis ads](https://substack.com/redirect/48b7a140-99d8-4910-9862-07f10b84b495?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) talking about his grandchildren was a masterstroke.
The "yes" campaign had an impressive ground game too: evidently they spent a lot of time at fraternities encouraging people to vote, since that's fertile ground for yes voters. (And, since it's all young men, it's fertile ground for gambling addicts--their best future customers.)
2
This effort got [shut down almost immediately](https://substack.com/redirect/47c04c7e-b4bf-4ca5-a3d8-da1167ca9f83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), at least for now.
3
Robinhood is, in fairness, not alone here. But they are the most prominent, and with a stock that's up about 5× in the last two years, they're clearly doing it well.
4
[A reminder](https://substack.com/redirect/1b916c7a-82ce-41b7-9f53-b672a7598ecf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): the American Psychiatry Association has a list of "Substance-Related and Addictive Disorders" that includes a lot of the culprits you'd expect: alcohol, meth, opioids, and more. [Gambling is the ](https://substack.com/redirect/70f2945e-d637-41c4-a179-396ad04a2571?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[only](https://substack.com/redirect/70f2945e-d637-41c4-a179-396ad04a2571?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ addiction that isn't directly related to substance use](https://substack.com/redirect/70f2945e-d637-41c4-a179-396ad04a2571?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Why? "Gambling disorder is similar to substance-related disorders in clinical expression, brain origin, comorbidity, physiology, and treatment."
5
Bankruptcies, for context, [have climbed by 28% in states where sports gambling is legal](https://substack.com/redirect/24cec087-c083-4a99-af7f-102da08e044d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
6
For further context: for every dollar people gamble on sports, [they put $2 less into an investment account](https://substack.com/redirect/0e11dee8-8e96-4ed8-ae3a-33fd32b726f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
7
This _Saturday Night Live_ parody ("[Rock Bottom Kings](https://substack.com/redirect/63226c1c-87e9-4d99-a1c9-f69d8945e2d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)") hits the nail on the head, particularly with Shane Gillis' impish smile when he notes that these companies take addiction seriously.
8
Robinhood [paid a $7.5 million fine last year](https://substack.com/redirect/d4144bd7-d604-4e60-8d18-2ab43e1dd48d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to resolve some of these complaints from Massachusetts. But that's hardly even a slap on the wrist in the context of a [company with a ~$50 billion market cap](https://substack.com/redirect/c8817f41-8461-4fd3-9c7a-1499eb6f4892?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
9
My thoughts: 15ish years on, it still makes absolutely zero sense to me.
10
This [same study](https://substack.com/redirect/adfe4703-8028-4c09-948f-aee22053a592?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) notes, "we found cryptocurrency traders share similar demographic and personality characteristics with share-traders and problem gamblers."
This has to be studied much more, but gambling and crypto share wild volatility and a get-rich-quick sort of attitude. Culturally, there's a certain machismo that both have. So it makes sense that there's a lot of overlap in the universe of people who gamble and who dabble in crypto.
11
Allowing the [$TRUMP meme coin](https://substack.com/redirect/d07e4bf8-47bb-4af5-b6ea-d631efb7723e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--which, like every meme coin, explicitly has no underlying economic value--[to trade on Robinhood](https://substack.com/redirect/64d2b030-c36a-4083-8f86-53a0c9057d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) cements that they're not serious about investing, and that they're really much more about gambling.
12
Yes, they do not charge commission. However: "Robinhood generates transaction-based revenues by routing its users' orders for options, equities, and cryptocurrencies to market makers, which is a process known as payment for order flow (PFOF)... The payment is usually only fractions of a penny per share but can be a significant source of revenue for companies dealing with a large number of orders." ([Source](https://substack.com/redirect/9791686b-024e-46a5-98f0-8ac8a097be03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg))
13
Robinhood [makes even more money from options trading](https://substack.com/redirect/ae229f6c-d89a-4cea-a702-860093cf24fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--[something they're increasingly encouraging its users to do](https://substack.com/redirect/f2918bb2-4fb5-4a8c-a296-f34872927119?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (Including in the UK, [literally as of yesterday](https://substack.com/redirect/7237eb84-9f76-4a9a-ac8a-ee1676a076bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).) This is its own category, but it's worth noting that options pricing is so complex that people have [literally won Nobel Prizes for their work on the theory of options pricing](https://substack.com/redirect/3460f190-b23c-41fa-a351-dbbf7796c45c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So no, the vast, vast majority of people shouldn't be options trading.
14
This isn't exactly the [hot hand fallacy](https://substack.com/redirect/8f05b52b-84ce-4939-9d9f-348def3d80e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), nor is it exactly the [gambler's fallacy](https://substack.com/redirect/6624933b-5d40-4b34-986b-9562c73c663e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It feels like it's something in between the two. Nonetheless, Robinhood is preying on dangerous psychological traps.
15
For instance, I tend to think that the [accredited investor rule](https://substack.com/redirect/bb35f93e-fdf7-4c46-a0b0-d63831b045a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is outdated and should be reformed.
16
The [data here](https://substack.com/redirect/9ad299d2-70c0-4c46-a7ad-d696a1e4001d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--"clinical director Jessica Steinmetz estimates about 10% of patients are seeking help for addictions tied to trading. Before 2020, there were no such patients"--is obviously pretty heavily anecdotal, as is other data in this _WSJ_ article. I wasn't able to find anything more robust or peer-reviewed, in part since it's such a difficult thing to measure. (For the purposes of measurement, it's not always obvious where the line exists between "investing responsibly" and "taking stupid risks as the result of addiction.")
But lots of data seems to back up that this is clearly a problem, and I hope that there's more robust data on this in the future.
17
Matt Levine from _Bloomberg_ [has written about how Robinhood and others](https://substack.com/redirect/9a22f1a9-3933-4392-92a5-c46060881c9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are profiting from this kind of reckless options trading that just amounts to gambling.
18
Retirement savings are a huge problem for women too, as they have [significantly less saved on average](https://substack.com/redirect/bb52fb51-e82e-403b-8d34-a6192359e61b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This is a critical issue, but the policy solutions are much different. Why? Women are, generally, less likely to gamble it away on a random NBA prop bet or, say, [Hawk Tuah Coin](https://substack.com/redirect/a71206f8-7b59-4197-891e-bc99c9818f94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (Yes, [people actually did that](https://substack.com/redirect/6960ae9c-4e1b-45fd-9552-ca6198fdcaf8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)
19
This was as of a couple years ago, at the height of the Robinhood-induced meme stock craze, though I would imagine numbers remain similar today.

Unlike Robin Hood, Robinhood steals from the poor , Robinhood CEO talked about the company's long-stated goal of opening up finance to everyone. In certain respects, that there's a lot about investing that isn't egalitarian: access to private markets, access to venture investing, etc. is limited.15 Democratizing investments would be good! But Robinhood isn 't doing that because they aren't promoting savings and investment. They're promoting gambling. It's no accident that Sports gambling is relatively easy to regulate; regulating how people gamble on platforms like Robinhood is much harder. Do we want to wholesale ban people from options trading? No, probably not. But do we want most Americans putting their retirement savings in options? Very clearly not.17 This TikTok video shows us on Robinhood. (Namely, by lying about your expertise.) And that, along with a million other issues, is a real problem. And even though returns for decades have demonstrated that it's part of a path to retirement for the . How do we solve this? 1. Regulation : Outlaw gamification that encourages big bets and irresponsible investments. Restrict notifications that encourage the sort of reactionary behavior that has people . 2. Separation : Don't allow people to invest and gamble (on sports or prediction markets) in the same account. It should not be that easy to move money from one to the other, and the two shouldn't be treated as equivalents. 3. Education : . Explain the power of compound interest. Help people understand that what they're seeing on Robinhood and elsewhere--something they're exposed to at an increasingly young age--is gambling, not investing. People will always want to get rich quick; that's an instinct as old as time. But the more we can do to keep people from recklessly gambling--something that's become a Robinhood forte--the better off we'll all be. ? name: The Editors
subject: Americans are Choosing Organizations that Reflect their Values

## ** _It No Longer Pays to Push Woke Politics. Americans are Choosing Organizations that Reflect their Values_**
---
| [](https://ssspl.mjt.lu/lnk/AU0AAGHRJ68AAc4R9vIAA5vbC1wAAYDOz7cAnf7RACZcGQBnrOdz2HUpxM3pSN6idtZvwDyu0QAjuaE/2/cFZvV-HNXE5GAkNsiCP6pA/aHR0cHM6Ly9wcmlja2x5cGVhci5uZXdzL2ZyZWUtYW1hYy1tZW1iZXJzaGlwLz9yZWY9bGliZXJ0eWxhYiZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249QW1lcmljYW5zJTIwYXJlJTIwQ2hvb3NpbmclMjBPcmdhbml6YXRpb25zJTIwdGhhdCUyMFJlZmxlY3QlMjB0aGVpciUyMFZhbHVlcyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9bWFpbGpldA)
---
In 2007, Dan Weber founded the Association of Mature American Citizens (AMAC) because he saw a glaring problem: **AARP had abandoned the values of millions of Americans who cherish faith, family, and freedom. While AARP cozied up to big government and leftist policies, conservatives were left without a voice—until AMAC stepped in.**

Fast forward to today, and the landscape is shifting faster than ever. The mood of the country is clear: Americans are rejecting businesses and organizations that pander to woke ideology at the expense of their customers. Companies that once believed they could endlessly alienate conservative consumers are learning the hard way—it simply doesn’t make business sense anymore. **Organizations that ignore their base in favor of fashionable activism are losing trust, loyalty, and most importantly, customers.**

Meanwhile, Washington, D.C. is moving at a breakneck pace, and the consequences of runaway spending, excessive government control, and progressive overreach are becoming undeniable. The recent elections sent a loud and clear message: Americans are fed up. They’re done with politicians, businesses, and organizations that prioritize virtue-signaling over real issues.

**That’s why AMAC isn’t just another membership group—it’s a movement.** We stand firm in our commitment to defending the principles that built this country. **And right now, we’re offering you the chance to join—for free. No credit card. No gimmicks. Just a full year of membership with access to exclusive benefits, valuable resources, and a community that shares your values.**

Why continue supporting organizations that don’t support you? The tide is turning—don’t get left behind. Join AMAC today for FREE and be part of a movement that puts conservative values first.
| [JOIN FOR FREE](https://ssspl.mjt.)
---
# **ABOUT THE PRICKLY PEAR**
The Prickly Pear ([_PricklyPear.news_](https://ssspl.mjt.lu/lnk/AU0AAGHRJ68AAc4R9vIAA5vbC1wAAYDOz7cAnf7RACZcGQBnrOdz2HUpxM3pSN6idtZvwDyu0QAjuaE/4/H-msi9_BsmOBgaaotkIRpw/aHR0cHM6Ly9wcmlja2x5cGVhci5uZXdzLz9yZWY9bGliZXJ0eWxhYiZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249QW1lcmljYW5zJTIwYXJlJTIwQ2hvb3NpbmclMjBPcmdhbml6YXRpb25zJTIwdGhhdCUyMFJlZmxlY3QlMjB0aGVpciUyMFZhbHVlcyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9bWFpbGpldA)) is focused on delivering timely, fact-based news, and citizen opinion that reflects our mission to “inform, educate and advocate about the principles of limited government and personal liberty.” [_Read our full mission statement here._](https://ssspl.mjt.)

To achieve that mission, Prickly Pear often engages with like-minded contributors and organizations who share our values. We encourage to support these partners in any way you can, as these partners make our efforts possible.

Thank you for your continued support.
| [VISIT OUR WEBSITE](https://ssspl.mjt.)

name: Sean Feucht
subject: NEW MUSIC VIDEO ALERT
body:
WATCH: His Shoulders (Live From Camp Elah) OFFICIAL MUSIC VIDEO| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=03db7113b0a94821befcfcb6580e5d85&_e=RKo2joqRSP6Wmz2o5LS4s9shJqp0aNBevQ7LSZe8cufwxy7PECaOqmvuWOkQdFQOAIavvuYk9SnAkQVxSlpQKBWOJfSGj7NPWRTK0JyhgtzgnyfvCGpLuWz2sMLp9SEM_VIBGVi-SP7dOfsFmfyuZ1GHP_CVcNq17yW-6nwPM_CALq7oVeV0UEG9PZ8uTj47FytZBP5X7yMP8NXp2sCv7wPOIRTs6grplu6DzhPUfyYa6A_q66G4_SbOhjI2JVrZc55gPYIZeni4y7RXJbilEzpBcQ5PPwfq3vAIkrruz_Eci12I4Xv1tlUnlSdIyvNvVOXBHNytsENGV_YTxX4aPA%3D%3D)
---
Dear Friend,
---
When the world goes crazy and politicians fail to deliver the solutions we seek, it’s critical to remember who sits on the throne and who is in control—JESUS!

Even before His birth, Isaiah prophesied of the authority Jesus would command: “And the government shall be upon His shoulder...”
“Of the increase of His government and of peace, there will be no end.”

Isaiah 9 is the inspiration behind our song, “His Shoulders,” which we recorded at our Washington, D.C. home base—Camp Elah! This intimate recording captures the passion of the song and the importance of remembering that the governments of the world rest on His shoulders! _**Watch His Shoulders (Live From Camp Elah) OFFICIAL MUSIC VIDEO**_

---
| | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=03db7113b0a94821befcfcb6580e5d85&_e=RKo2joqRSP6Wmz2o5LS4s9shJqp0aNBevQ7LSZe8cufwxy7PECaOqmvuWOkQdFQOAIavvuYk9SnAkQVxSlpQKBWOJfSGj7NPWRTK0JyhgtzgnyfvCGpLuWz2sMLp9SEM_VIBGVi-SP7dOfsFmfyuZ1GHP_CVcNq17yW-6nwPM_CALq7oVeV0UEG9PZ8uTj47UyGYNb_J4jurp80vzWZRvq_3ZeKtpx4VpV35YsfelKK1xHkTc4SfsGEy1XO97t-WLkSoQKtQfgT85Xu2Z9Q_rG1mBJkHly4j91qkHsmFUrJfxv1_sctRZOfRMX-nDsi7UpzWBIX3vWw8yY0iNvpmTA%3D%3D)
---
| | 🎶 The government is resting on His shoulders
The government is resting on His shoulders
Jesus, the name above all others
The government is resting on His shoulders 🎶
---
Till all hear,
---
---
Sean
---

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: Elon Musk shouldn’t have access to your personal data
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102010656/527795216/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVhNjU2ZC02ZmU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZ7pQNEo7v4poAnGLLZn03MU9qgb0YYinBJKmzwmYoA=&emci=692eb44f-58e9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=14ea656d-6fe9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=13185894) Derek,

Over the last couple weeks, I have heard from people across Western Pennsylvania who are worried and angry about their personal, private data and information being in the hands of Elon Musk’s crew.

Let me be direct with you Derek: Elon Musk—with his massive conflicts of interest—shouldn’t have access to the Treasury payment system. He shouldn’t have access to your Social Security numbers or your bank account information.

**That’s why I’m supporting the Taxpayer Data Protection Act. We’ve got to put an end to this unfettered access to private information.** **[We need to stop Elon Musk and his crew’s free rein over our personal information. Please stand with me in this fight and sign my petition telling Congress to pass the Taxpayer Data Protection Act →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102010657/527795218/-902989426?refcode=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&source=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&utm_campaign=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVhNjU2ZC02ZmU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZ7pQNEo7v4poAnGLLZn03MU9qgb0YYinBJKmzwmYoA=&emci=692eb44f-58e9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=14ea656d-6fe9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=13185894)** | | [Sign the petition →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102010658/527795219/-902989426?refcode=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&source=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&utm_campaign=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVhNjU2ZC02ZmU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZ7pQNEo7v4poAnGLLZn03MU9qgb0YYinBJKmzwmYoA=&emci=692eb44f-58e9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=14ea656d-6fe9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=13185894)
---
The bill would make it clear that only properly vetted officials can access the Treasury payment system—halting Musk's and DOGE's access.

We only need three Republicans to stand with us in support of this bill to get it passed in the House. So we need to show them that people in Cresco and across the nation want to stop this reckless invasion of privacy.

**[Add your name to my petition and tell Congress to pass the Taxpayer Data Protection Act →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102010659/527795221/-902989426?refcode=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&source=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&utm_campaign=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVhNjU2ZC02ZmU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZ7pQNEo7v4poAnGLLZn03MU9qgb0YYinBJKmzwmYoA=&emci=692eb44f-58e9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=14ea656d-6fe9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=13185894)**
| [Sign the petition →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102010660/527795224/-902989426?refcode=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&source=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&utm_campaign=email_021225_taxpayerdataelon&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVhNjU2ZC02ZmU5LWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CZ7pQNEo7v4poAnGLLZn03MU9qgb0YYinBJKmzwmYoA=&emci=692eb44f-58e9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=14ea656d-6fe9-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=13185894)

Chris

subject: This new image says it all

| Dear Hello Today's big news is that the Virginia Senate passed the House Budget Bill [HB1600](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZu_vWnPDheSTTno5r97Cs4fUwh6_vUnLlnK_CMJ1qiHUJrxdpOp8FpyGz5-FjOAEUcUWuE1ccvcu7BSj-F598yRYRLsfHMSGjAAEqMaldS-frHtN-X8yD01lkLegYjyJf1Xf5mPzt09e&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==) with support from both Republicans and Democrats 37-2-1. I've posted the vote for you below. Republican Senator Bill Stanley abstained.
Personally, I would have voted against the bill. Way to much spending on non-core functions of government. Kudos to Senators Sturtevant and Cifers for voting "no."
Now the House budget bill and the Senate budget bill will head to a conference committee of a handful of Democrat and Republican House and Senate conferees who will hash out the difference between the bill. And then a final vote will take place in both Houses.
That said, the General Assembly should finish their work on time by the Saturday, February 22 deadline.
---
| |
---
Next Saturday, February 22 is the last day of the 2025 Legislative Session. Then the Governor has until Monday, March 24 to veto, sign into law or offer amendments to bills that passed both the House and the Senate.
I predict Governor Youngkin will set another all time high veto record as he is the only red brick wall we have to block all of the authoritarian Democrat bills. This is the last year, we have this guarantee.
Once session is over next Saturday, we must focus on November elections this coming fall for Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Attorney General and the Virginia House.
So stay tuned.

### [HB1552 Critical access hospitals; swing beds.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZiyyHKIVTrgYILnZgWrpi8UvdINJSDR4Z1MvkEIGpJk4PnBLBQTyCTMkGAHjGPSj9iZOoS-lNmyTk-vMzF4x4Ceu27Qwngbiuvcyp4MyvuZiyQN4GhBtvJbw0HYUQwWy_VHGEk3qvMp-&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==)
Introduced by: [Bill Wiley (Chief Patron)](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZiyyHKIVTrgYFBBPam4xdudVENOKdIRF0s2vGuBFTyv4LLltIEhDiqQ3WU4woEW7XVBbEWu-JmBKJBH7gYBPYssLCFnD9l5ZEqXViDWp-prAUpPLM8JmITbISQriQXUsKFixzWAuJ11CklXkYUdB4AUjY_KFvqw6uavX6sYwCP6ddrlx4T_xKbbDDkxUkyBWkA==&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==) Certificate of public need; exception; critical access hospitals; swing beds; sunset. Allows critical access hospitals to utilize an average of up to 10 swing beds per day, calculated over the hospital's fiscal year, without obtaining a certificate of public need. Under current law, critical access hospitals may only use up to 10 percent of beds as swing beds. The bill directs the State Commissioner of Health to collect data annually from critical access hospitals that use fiscal year averaging for swing beds and make such data publicly available. The bill has an expiration date of July 1, 2028.

### [HB1649 Board of Medicine; continuing education; unconscious bias and cultural competency.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZnWg00W5tYNlXlJL6JBokKqgeyX9TLvLM2EeoJXAEkBKd981xKDJhMuNkaAb-r9lX_5oKaR1hjW1RnDmOAFAXh6o8f_7vAhq8vYrNRLMbk1SV45j3B2H0JVRNyo-VQPY2fBtepeqQjTO&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==)
Introduced by: [C.E. Cliff Hayes, Jr. (Chief Patron)](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZnWg00W5tYNlIgQksOYj4UmKGsjNR60giLOwql2f9qZTXnZGfAep2_56X7qKtXtVRG1bGUEpmcNqmk3_v__7OQ0bMRgZa2JVVQaE2jGpEO49tycdNN9HRqKDC_Arq19KEZsqlHhSKX7cTa7m1sRExZie_D6OFW6pRMjYcfoqJ2btW-CV3M3H4640brmmW78hgw==&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==) Board of Medicine; continuing education; unconscious bias and cultural competency. Directs the Board of Medicine to require unconscious bias and cultural competency training as part of the continuing education requirements for renewal of licensure. The bill specifies requirements for the training and requires the Board of Medicine to report on the training to the Department of Health and the Virginia Neonatal Perinatal Collaborative.

### [HB1678 School board policies; parental notification; safe storage of prescription drugs and firearms in the household.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZnWg00W5tYNlFBjjfpRXR7Os64TO6LYdkMYVVhl1PGsa-hrp3T0s9csYPNxn1GcMJEO68lksBHrscZL2uqgxtGIrRMPY2_K3Oq1CDYkZF7RKwlGSgMOYvyZIm9Lms5evqK2O7jHcacyX&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==)
Introduced by: [Laura Jane Cohen (Chief Patron)](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZnWg00W5tYNl3VmogjX_J8m0_uBzAwvQB5rsVOmalzNdZ1OyWuOaRb_WwPyQhEdaHp1D8WCWL6j6S5-NXNMdFuI4Tnsj9YOMoAkuBXowcE7P4gcd2HgB5M5PDJjvvSK6jz3ZdgqA3aQcZ0s2LZtA7Woy4A6AMmTsXkyvbhNhI4tUbHCQv5dzvMxgDrKjFA-xag==&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==) School board policies; parental notification; safe storage of prescription drugs and firearms in the household. Requires each local school board to develop and implement a policy to require the annual notification of the parent of each student enrolled in the local school division, to be sent by email and, if applicable, SMS text message within 30 calendar days succeeding the first day of each school year, of (i) the importance of securely storing any prescription drug, as defined in relevant law, present in the household and (ii) the parent's legal responsibility to safely store any firearm present in the household. The bill requires each school board to make such parental notification available in multiple languages on its website.

### [HB1960 Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person.](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZnWg00W5tYNlA66KpQk8Fq_GFc9qPiEhuUuEAOQBiNlwwIhwKzy-5XdWfGCU5tsRfjUEO0gUU8nGl-LcwH8mnmdV6Q4Fwd3lujGiAkstTVY6HF5gUbXdS7Sa_D54INsQnY5t7j7V68qm&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==)
Introduced by: [Elizabeth B. Bennett-Parker (Chief Patron)](https://byh4wqwab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-2qkzJUae4-bwCxD5ncLXHCCQqjl9F3EbcKrWCouMOUwNe-F1RBaZiyyHKIVTrgY_2xZjVfsqosEtjqoTjQ_ZshO3-io3Sx-n3_9X5QKJ_95R41gxZmAQsqwF2Mg2fZTnp66XkhS0tAPZy6YdK1FJTFZsNOQaLJbKGnANjdBbFRylbTkKziLH1bVKNz8KvbQ4vgW-QgK3yqV6zyhktrxbs21MCLGwpqjzQAgmz4WRHdobERx5pns8g==&c=i294kLp2Gt3C0PzN84p0UXXmya6vvayIuj1HWcdq76p-t0orPTpciQ==&ch=XBQfNwPCh0a0_6AVnNBfZeIP_grILsTBEHaf93GqHdY8i3DtNIgg9w==) Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm and shall provide a copy of such form to the transferee. The bill also provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member shall be advised that a law-enforcement officer may obtain a search warrant to search for any firearms from such person if such law-enforcement officer has reason to believe that such person has not relinquished all firearms in his possession.

| In the Virginia Senate, each bill has three Constitutional reads. Only bills on the third read and are on the "regular calendar" are debated.
Bills that are on the "uncontested calendar," are bills that all Democrat and Republican Senators gave a thumbs up. So, when the bill comes to the Senator floor from the committee, unless a Senator pulls the bill out of the block, the bill is not debated on the floor and is simply voted on in a block.
As a Senator, I always reviewed the bills on third read on the uncontested calendar and many times would pull a bill out of the block, to hear the patron present the bill, to ensure I was in agreement with the members of the committee.
I've only highlighted bills on the third read for your convenience.
---
|
# THE BUDGET
---
| During the 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate, I can honestly say I can only remember voting for one of the budgets, and that was called, "the skinny bill." This was a bill that was considered a temporary measure until we could meet again. It only funded the core functions of government, which is what I think is the only expenditure that should be included in the budget.
I'm glad to see my former Senate opponent's Sturtevant and Cifers followed my lead on this. I've highlighted the Senators by party using blue for a Democrat Senator and red for a Republican Senator.
Although we are required by law to pass a balanced budget every year, unlike the federal government, there is far too much spending on non-core functions of government to include DEI and corporate welfare. I think Senators Sturtevant and Cifers made the right move in voting against this budget with amendments.
---
|

NAYS\--Cifers, Sturtevant--2 RULE 36--0 NOT VOTING--Stanley--1

clean_body: The 2025 General Assembly Session | | | | 2025 THE HONEY BADGER REPORT | | | 2025 SESSION Thursday What can you expect? | | Thursday Senate Committee Meetings | ATTEND IN PERSON OR CALL IN TO THE MEETING LIVE | == == == == == == == == == == == == == | AMANDA CHASE | VIRGINIA'S HONEY BADGER | SESSION BEGINS AT NOON | Dear Hello Today's big news is that the Virginia Senate passed the House Budget Bill == with support from both Republicans and Democrats 37-2-1. I've posted the vote for you below. Republican Senator Bill Stanley abstained. Personally, I would have voted against the bill. Way to much spending on non-core functions of government. Kudos to Senators Sturtevant and Cifers for voting "no." Now the House budget bill and the Senate budget bill will head to a conference committee of a handful of Democrat and Republican House and Senate conferees who will hash out the difference between the bill. And then a final vote will take place in both Houses. That said, the General Assembly should finish their work on time by the Saturday, February 22 deadline. | | Next Saturday, February 22 is the last day of the 2025 Legislative Session. Then the Governor has until Monday, March 24 to veto, sign into law or offer amendments to bills that passed both the House and the Senate. I predict Governor Youngkin will set another all time high veto record as he is the only red brick wall we have to block all of the authoritarian Democrat bills. This is the last year, we have this guarantee. Once session is over next Saturday, we must focus on November elections this coming fall for Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Attorney General and the Virginia House. So stay tuned. | THIS IS WHAT HAPPENS WHEN DEMOCRATS ARE IN CHARGE DELEGATE NICK FRETAS FLOOR SPEECH | | == | HIGHLIGHTS HOUSE BILLS THAT PASSED BOTH HOUSES | == Introduced by: == Certificate of public need; exception; critical access hospitals; swing beds; sunset. Allows critical access hospitals to utilize an average of up to 10 swing beds per day, calculated over the hospital's fiscal year, without obtaining a certificate of public need. Under current law, critical access hospitals may only use up to 10 percent of beds as swing beds. The bill directs the State Commissioner of Health to collect data annually from critical access hospitals that use fiscal year averaging for swing beds and make such data publicly available. The bill has an expiration date of July 1, 2028. == Introduced by: == Board of Medicine; continuing education; unconscious bias and cultural competency. Directs the Board of Medicine to require unconscious bias and cultural competency training as part of the continuing education requirements for renewal of licensure. The bill specifies requirements for the training and requires the Board of Medicine to report on the training to the Department of Health and the Virginia Neonatal Perinatal Collaborative. == Introduced by: == School board policies; parental notification; safe storage of prescription drugs and firearms in the household. Requires each local school board to develop and implement a policy to require the annual notification of the parent of each student enrolled in the local school division, to be sent by email and, if applicable, SMS text message within 30 calendar days succeeding the first day of each school year, of (i) the importance of securely storing any prescription drug, as defined in relevant law, present in the household and (ii) the parent's legal responsibility to safely store any firearm present in the household. The bill requires each school board to make such parental notification available in multiple languages on its website. == Introduced by: == Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm and shall provide a copy of such form to the transferee. The bill also provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member shall be advised that a law-enforcement officer may obtain a search warrant to search for any firearms from such person if such law-enforcement officer has reason to believe that such person has not relinquished all firearms in his possession. | | == | == == == | In the Virginia Senate, each bill has three Constitutional reads. Only bills on the third read and are on the "regular calendar" are debated. Bills that are on the "uncontested calendar," are bills that all Democrat and Republican Senators gave a thumbs up. So, when the bill comes to the Senator floor from the committee, unless a Senator pulls the bill out of the block, the bill is not debated on the floor and is simply voted on in a block. As a Senator, I always reviewed the bills on third read on the uncontested calendar and many times would pull a bill out of the block, to hear the patron present the bill, to ensure I was in agreement with the members of the committee. I've only highlighted bills on the third read for your convenience. | THE BUDGET | During the 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate, I can honestly say I can only remember voting for one of the budgets, and that was called, "the skinny bill." This was a bill that was considered a temporary measure until we could meet again. It only funded the core functions of government, which is what I think is the only expenditure that should be included in the budget. I'm glad to see my former Senate opponent's Sturtevant and Cifers followed my lead on this. I've highlighted the Senators by party using blue for a Democrat Senator and red for a Republican Senator. Although we are required by law to pass a balanced budget every year, unlike the federal government, there is far too much spending on non-core functions of government to include DEI and corporate welfare. I think Senators Sturtevant and Cifers made the right move in voting against this budget with amendments. | HB1600 Budget Bill. 02/12/2025 Senate: Passed Senate with amendments (37-Y 2-N) Votes YEAS\--Aird, Bagby, Boysko, Carroll Foy, Craig, Deeds, DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Ebbin, Favola, French, Hackworth, Hashmi, Head, Jordan, Locke, Lucas, Marsden, McDougle, McPike, Mulchi, Obenshain, Peake, Pekarsky, Perry, Pillion, Reeves, Roem, Rouse, Salim, Srinivasan, Stuart, Suetterlein, Surovell, VanValkenburg, Williams Graves--37 NAYS\--Cifers, Sturtevant--2 RULE 36--0 NOT VOTING--Stanley--1 I've included the links below to the original budget bill, along with the budget amendments for your review. | | == | | == | | | | | | | | | IMPORTANT DATES | 2025 LEGISLATIVE SESSION IMPORTANT DATES Begins: Wednesday, January 8 at noon Crossover: Tuesday, February 4 Ends: Saturday, February 22 Last Day for Governor's Actions: Monday, March 24 Reconvene: Wednesday, April 2 | IMPORTANT LINKS | | == | | == | | == | == | | == | SUPPORT AMANDA HERE | While this publication is free, your contributions are greatly appreciated. I served 8 years in the Virginia Senate from 2016-2023 and want to put that experience and knowledge to work for you. I know how things really work in Richmond and I want you to know too, so that you can hold your elected representatives accountable. A huge thanks to those of you who have already chipped in. Thank you. We must now save Virginia. Let's do this. | | == | | == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- == | == ---|--- | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US | | | | | | |

The GOP-backed candidate in the Wisconsin Supreme Court race, Judge Brad Schimel, launched a $1.4 million ad campaign last month. Schimel, who’s supported the state’s extreme 1849 abortion ban and defended egregiously gerrymandered maps in court, is facing off against Judge Susan Crawford. The Democrats are losing their minds over this: |
---
Peter,

The Democrats are __**losing their minds**__ over this:

[
](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=32f3ad106e2d4e2a8bfc4e7ee1b44394&_e=UM_2BMPWieFAVHL25hzJxddwgO0lx_FHgvv5LShTvJmT5u_3PivTZlJovTJFKBrfRlwzfaJv2SGaahIemcHDTMe2HFa2egmTvTh_RxlW-G_zpA22S3a2hlXqC9NwhD1t9pGq2AJhtAvusoTOJ6NSAX-hg6glINlvN0zpN4kLKEYDCRfr29eGsmQ4UGijY-y_koi2j7DPTn8D_WY4YiflWsMlDg2c1z7Ri0mjqygKoxdaq0S3Fbv4hJzYmRnDymgy94X8dJ40pn4LQIJ6LSAYGFU-GtLm-CxfL-aesa9RZ2wSyHtc5OIrmDxIA1Ia8IFiS-VJ5Q-fiXpLfvxbrKYe22mImZfKJPi2aAWve5ZXloEf-SJCcjEyV-BXGGIUM_RFna87mDEyBgzVoRh8VO7ku7MwT5PdaJIlZSLeaG0RlJs%3D)
**President Trump is doing exactly what he promised on the campaign trail: gutting the Deep State, draining the swamp, and Making America Great Again.**

The Left can’t stand it.

They can’t stand that their hidden woke agenda’s funding is being cut, federal workers are getting back to work, and we are making government smaller -- not bigger. It’s simple, Peter:

**_Cut the waste._**
**_Audit the spending._**
**_End the woke policies._**
[**GUT THE DEEP STATE!**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=32f3ad106e2d4e2a8bfc4e7ee1b44394&_e=UM_2BMPWieFAVHL25hzJxddwgO0lx_FHgvv5LShTvJmT5u_3PivTZlJovTJFKBrfRlwzfaJv2SGaahIemcHDTMe2HFa2egmTvTh_RxlW-G_zpA22S3a2hlXqC9NwhD1t9pGq2AJhtAvusoTOJ6NSAX-hg6glINlvN0zpN4kLKEYDCRfr29eGsmQ4UGijY-y_koi2j7DPTn8D_WY4YiflWsMlDg2c1z7Ri0mjqygKoxdaq0S3Fbv4hJzYmRnDymgy94X8dJ40pn4LQIJ6LSAYGCrHLShekxQLpD_8dfgaAMFKLJaj_tEihZsJXWM4PPDBMjZR284Rvsno6wNjuku35JnPSSxRsXpu1BB6GZVdKLFekyvMRH6y1ZuplFFdb49_EPyX1z2xpe4Onno0qros5k-wIeEw95OLetcUhEubbBU%3D)

With Marco Rubio as Secretary of State, Pam Bondi as Attorney General, and Mike Waltz as the National Security Advisor -- these three Floridians are leading the way in Washington, gutting the Deep State day by day.

[**Peter, if you agree that the woke ideology must come to an end and the Swamp is past due for draining, please let me know NOW!**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=32f3ad106e2d4e2a8bfc4e7ee1b44394&_e=UM_2BMPWieFAVHL25hzJxddwgO0lx_FHgvv5LShTvJmT5u_3PivTZlJovTJFKBrfRlwzfaJv2SGaahIemcHDTMe2HFa2egmTvTh_RxlW-G_zpA22S3a2hlXqC9NwhD1t9pGq2AJhtAvusoTOJ6NSAX-hg6glINlvN0zpN4kLKEYDCRfr29eGsmQ4UGijY-y_koi2j7DPTn8D_WY4YiflWsMlDg2c1z7Ri0mjqygKoxdaq0S3Fbv4hJzYmRnDymgy94X8dJ40pn4LQIJ6LSAYGJFPXKhl70VVotdpWqKNOJ2y_4kDIspuiVJhKBTeaWd-m9CHjlgigc42Ui3S6kSe49VdGduu3ev9wmYwNWnJRBjxeqoLQnod_BXJg80OJORtKik12jHXFoGySwMmZp5OfxtR6m9NXS9ca8KPVWDb0yY%3D)

[**YES, I AGREE!**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=32f3ad106e2d4e2a8bfc4e7ee1b44394&_e=UM_2BMPWieFAVHL25hzJxddwgO0lx_FHgvv5LShTvJmT5u_3PivTZlJovTJFKBrfRlwzfaJv2SGaahIemcHDTMe2HFa2egmTvTh_RxlW-G_zpA22S3a2hlXqC9NwhD1t9pGq2AJhtAvusoTOJ6NSAX-hg6glINlvN0zpN4kLKEYDCRfr29eGsmQ4UGijY-y_koi2j7DPTn8D_WY4YiflWsMlDg2c1z7Ri0mjqygKoxdaq0S3Fbv4hJzYmRnDymgy94X8dJ40pn4LQIJ6LSAYGJBInJCBtB_sBoIplSMjlqZxn4ThPzuUyH2yaX4GkIEfx2Ugsc0Qrijs3aVtbr5nnY3yigdeODx_JETmo6oQIHH4f5B0sVaqG5-QUbBX-TKgWcC2JAC83giIDvoTyYc82yy2KFbllQ5bS83yb6c6FQM%3D)

* * *
The Republican Party of Florida will promote the principles upon which our nation and our state were founded: freedom, liberty, personal responsibility, and accountability.
